patch.value:
## Context

File: src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts lines 528-555 — resolveTelemetrySourceContext returns source_context. PM_AUTHOR env var is not read here.

File: src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts lines 818-871 — buildCommandStartPayload; no author_context field.

The source_context='automation' bucket currently conflates all non-TTY invocations: CI, MCP, agent loops, scripts. Hashing PM_AUTHOR (same HMAC-SHA256 keyed on installation_id as used for pm_root_hash) would be privacy-preserving but allow counting distinct agent types.

## Acceptance criteria

- When PM_AUTHOR env var is a non-empty string, include a stable `author_context_hash` field (HMAC-SHA256, keyed on installation_id) in command_start and command_finish payloads at capture_level != minimal.
- At minimal capture_level, include only a boolean `has_author_context: true/false`.
- No raw PM_AUTHOR value is ever included.
- SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS check is not needed (it's a hash); document this in a comment.
- Unit test: same PM_AUTHOR value + same installation_id = same hash; different installation_id = different hash.

## Evidence

src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:52 — source_context values list; no 'agent' tier.
src/core/telemetry/runtime.ts:859-870 — runtime object in payload; no PM_AUTHOR dimension.
Add agent-identity dimension to telemetry events: surface PM_AUTHOR as hashed author_context

Telemetry events record source_context (user/automation/test/dogfood/audit_smoke) and session_id but carry no signal identifying agent-driven invocations vs. human CLI use within the 'automation' bucket. PM_AUTHOR (e.g. 'maintainer-agent', 'claude-code-agent') is already set on mutations; exposing a hashed derivative in telemetry payloads would allow Sentry/dashboard queries to segment agent-driven usage patterns, error rates, and latency without leaking the raw author string.
author_context_hash in command_start/finish payloads when PM_AUTHOR is set; never contains raw string; minimal-level falls back to has_author_context boolean; tests cover hash stability and privacy.

- When PM_AUTHOR env var is a non-empty string, include a stable `author_context_hash` field (HMAC-SHA256, keyed on installation_id) in command_start and command_finish payloads at capture_level != minimal.
- At minimal capture_level, include only a boolean `has_author_context: true/false`.
- No raw PM_AUTHOR value is ever included.
- SENSITIVE_KEYWORDS check is not needed (it's a hash); document this in a comment.
- Unit test: same PM_AUTHOR value + same installation_id = same hash; different installation_id = different hash.
